The '''Force Web''', a network of filaments that connected [[Galaxy|galaxies]], was the largest known structure in the [[universe]]<ref name="The Glass Abyss" /> by [[32 BBY]].<ref name="Date">The events of ''[[Mace Windu: The Glass Abyss]]'' take place the [[Standard week|week]] after the [[funeral of Qui-Gon Jinn]], which ''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates to [[32 BBY]].</ref> Shortly before that time, [[Galactic Republic]] [[scientist]]s had announced the discovery of the Force Web, the study of which they hoped would yield information about the formation and evolution of galaxies.<ref name="The Glass Abyss" /> In that [[Standard year|year]],<ref name="Date" /> [[Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu]] thought about the Force Web after realizing the nature of the [[mycelia]]l web that connected the [[planet]] [[Metagos]] through [[the Force]].<ref name="The Glass Abyss" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Force Web was mentioned in the [[2024]] novel ''[[Mace Windu: The Glass Abyss]]'', written by [[Steven Barnes]].<ref name="The Glass Abyss" /> In the real world, the [[Wikipedia:Observable universe#Large-scale structure|cosmic web]] is the large-scale structure that connects [[Wikipedia:Galaxy|galaxies]], [[Wikipedia:Supercluster|superclusters]], and [[Wikipedia:Galaxy filament|galactic walls]].<ref name="NASA">{{WebCite|url=https://science.nasa.gov/universe/galaxies/large-scale-structures|text=Large Scale Structures - NASA Science|work=[[Wikipedia:NASA|NASA]]|archivedate=20241009145959}}</ref>
